transcript:
Here's what you're watching: a dark field where faint grey ellipses overlap like an unlit constellation, and tiny ochre dots slowly, patiently trace themselves onto those curves until the hidden shape becomes undeniable. Nothing snaps into place — it accretes.

Look at how those dots move. In the update function, each one called a "witness" is checking its distance to every boundary and behaving differently depending on which side it's on — pulled tangentially along the curve, gently repelled if it strays inside, gently drawn back if it drifts outside. The comment calls this "attention gathering where it's held." There's no dot that "belongs" to a shape — the shape is just the statistical residue of where witnesses keep returning.

And notice the boundaries never draw themselves brighter on their own — opacity only rises with "avgPressure," a value that accumulates from how often witnesses linger nearby, then decays if they leave. The structure isn't asserting itself. It's being verified into visibility by attention paid to it, over and over, until proof outlasts doubt.
